text.tspan: The assessment is the moat. The redesign earns it.
Current state of the live assessment, the gaps worth closing, and five comparable intake tools mined for moves worth borrowing.
What the live assessment does today, and where it falls short.
The user must agree to all five sections
percentage. It does not show which sections
are done, which were skipped, or what is left.
Output is a tier label and a generic /learn
link. No per-section breakdown. No specific
Score jumps in with no transition. Micro-moments matter.
Highest-value action is only reachable from the homepage CTA.
Foot health changes over months. The current product is a one-shot.
Pain is interoceptive. Forcing yes / no produces false positives.
Five comparable intake tools. The move worth borrowing, and the move worth avoiding.
Borrow: full-screen single-Q pattern. Avoid: 20+ screens
before any value. Heavy length kills the same audience
Borrow: a "why we ask" microcopy per question. Builds
trust during data collection. Useful for the pain section
where the user might wonder why.
Borrow: triage-first. User picks concerns, then only
relevant sections render. Closest match to what MSR
Borrow: each path is short. Five questions max per
concern. Avoid: hard product sell at the end. MSR keeps
Borrow: "we will check in at 14 days" framing for re-
engagement. Direct analog for the 30 / 90 day pattern
Function Health pattern. Reduces median time and irrelevant section drop-off.
BetterHelp pattern, used sparingly. Default stays multi-Q per section, single-Q for the pain section.
WHOOP pattern. Used on the pain section to explain the "Not sure" option.
Hims pattern. Each section caps at five to seven items max.
Eight Sleep pattern. 30 and 90 day automated re-check emails.
